back_translation: The LORD said to Moses, 'Go to Pharaoh and speak to him: Thus says the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, Let my people go that they may serve me.'
'But if you refuse to let them go and continue to hold them,'
'behold, the hand of the LORD will bring a very severe plague on your livestock that are in the field — on the horses, donkeys, camels, herds, and flocks.'
'The LORD will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt; not one belonging to the sons of Israel shall die.'
The LORD set an appointed time, saying, 'Tomorrow the LORD will do this thing in the land.'
The next day the LORD did this thing; all the livestock of the Egyptians died, but not one of the livestock of the sons of Israel died.
Pharaoh sent, and behold, not one of the livestock of Israel had died. But the heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the people go.
The LORD said to Moses and Aaron, 'Take handfuls of soot from the furnace, and let Moses toss it toward heaven in the sight of Pharaoh.'
'It shall become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and shall become boils breaking out in sores on man and beast throughout the land of Egypt.'
They took soot from the furnace and stood before Pharaoh; Moses tossed it toward heaven, and it became boils breaking out in sores on man and beast.
The magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils, for the boils were on the magicians and on all the Egyptians.
But the LORD hardened the heart of Pharaoh, and he did not listen to them, just as the LORD had spoken to Moses.
The LORD said to Moses, 'Rise early in the morning and stand before Pharaoh, and say to him: Thus says the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, Let my people go that they may serve me.'
'For this time I am sending all my plagues upon your heart, your servants, and your people, so that you may know that there is none like me in all the earth.'
'For by now I would have stretched out my hand and struck you and your people with plague, and you would have been wiped off the earth.'
'But for this purpose I have raised you up, to show you my power, and that my name might be declared in all the earth.'
'You still exalt yourself over my people, not letting them go.'
'Behold, about this time tomorrow I will cause to rain a very heavy hail, such as has not been in Egypt from the day it was founded until now.'

'Now send and shelter your livestock and all that you have in the field; every man and beast that is found in the field and is not gathered into the house — the hail shall come down on them, and they shall die.'
Those of Pharaoh's servants who feared the word of the LORD made their servants and livestock flee into the houses.
But those who did not set their heart on the word of the LORD left their servants and livestock in the field.
The LORD said to Moses, 'Stretch out your hand toward heaven, that there may be hail in all the land of Egypt — on man, beast, and every plant of the field in the land of Egypt.'
Moses stretched out his staff toward heaven, and the LORD gave thunder and hail; fire ran down to the earth. The LORD rained hail upon the land of Egypt.
There was hail, and fire flashing in the midst of the hail, very heavy, such as had not been in all the land of Egypt since it became a nation.
The hail struck throughout all the land of Egypt every thing that was in the field, both man and beast; the hail struck every plant of the field and shattered every tree of the field.
Only in the land of Goshen, where the sons of Israel were, there was no hail.
Pharaoh sent and called Moses and Aaron, and said to them, 'This time I have sinned; the LORD is righteous, and I and my people are wicked.'
'Pray to the LORD, for there has been enough of God's thunder and hail. I will let you go, and you shall not stay any longer.'
Moses said to him, 'When I go out of the city, I will spread out my hands to the LORD; the thunder shall cease and there shall be no more hail, that you may know that the earth belongs to the LORD.'
'But as for you and your servants, I know that you do not yet fear the LORD God.'
(The flax and the barley were struck, for the barley was ripe and the flax was in bud.)
(But the wheat and the spelt were not struck, for they are late crops.)
Moses went out from Pharaoh, out of the city, and spread out his hands to the LORD; the thunder and hail ceased, and the rain no longer poured upon the earth.
When Pharaoh saw that the rain, hail, and thunder had ceased, he sinned again and hardened his heart — he and his servants.
The heart of Pharaoh was hardened, and he did not let the sons of Israel go, just as the LORD had spoken through Moses.
